dpa_trigger_rows.text:
ID: A-01
Trigger Condition: Vendor will process personal data of EU/EEA data subjects on behalf of Lichen
Data Types Involved: Any personal data of EU/EEA individuals (name, email, ID, behavioral, device identifiers)
Regulations Triggered: GDPR Art. 28; GDPR Art. 4(8)
DPA Required?: REQUIRED
Required DPA / Contract Clauses: GDPR Art. 28 DPA; mandatory clauses: subject matter & duration; nature & purpose of processing; data type & data subject categories; obligations & rights of controller
Additional Obligations: Lichen Legal must execute Art. 28 DPA before any processing begins.
Lichen Action / Owner: Owner: General Counsel / Privacy Team

ID: A-06
Trigger Condition: Vendor processes only operational scheduling metadata derived from EU employee shift patterns, with no persistent storage of personally identifiable fields and processing limited to aggregate statistical outputs
Data Types Involved: Derived scheduling metrics, shift-pattern aggregates, anonymized operational KPIs — no direct personal identifiers retained by vendor
Regulations Triggered: GDPR Recital 26 (anonymization exclusion); GDPR Art. 2(1) material scope limitation
DPA Required?: NOT REQUIRED
Required DPA / Contract Clauses: None — processing falls outside GDPR material scope when limited to anonymized operational outputs with no persistent PII storage
Additional Obligations: Vendor must attest in writing that no personally identifiable employee data is retained beyond the aggregation step. Lichen Privacy Team must verify the anonymization methodology before engagement.
Lichen Action / Owner: Owner: Privacy Team (verification only)

nda_clause_chunks.text: 12.1.4 Non-Disclosure Agreement Requirement. A fully executed non-disclosure agreement (NDA) must be in place between Lichen Manufacturing and the third-party vendor before any exchange of confidential information, proprietary data, or access credentials may occur. No information exchange is permitted prior to NDA execution. The NDA must cover the full scope of data expected to be shared during the vendor engagement, including technical architecture details, employee data schemas, and integration specifications.
